local_acceptance_layer.purpose: Validate bounded activation order, reviewed triage gating, normalized consumer-readiness, and ownership limits.

notes: Use these cases as local acceptance gates first.
When this activation slice is evaluated as orchestration or tool-use quality, align the metric vocabulary with agent-tool-benchmark instead of inventing a separate scoring layer.
Keep benchmark alignment bounded to the reviewed activation slice rather than downstream worksheet, RAG, or rollout surfaces.

activation_slice_interpretation.AST Accuracy: Exact structural correctness of reviewed triage, bounded activation state, and consumer-readiness decisions.
activation_slice_interpretation.API-Bank L1: Exact correctness of required parser, normalization, and wrapper-readiness surfaces within the bounded activation slice.
activation_slice_interpretation.F1 Score (Set): Correct set membership for reviewed candidate images, bounded parser-smoke inputs, and normalized consumer artifacts.
activation_slice_interpretation.3-Milestone AND: Reviewed triage, parser-and-normalization evidence, and bounded consumer smoke all succeed together.
activation_slice_interpretation.Resolve Rate: The slice reaches a usable reviewed terminal state without scope widening or truth-source drift.

evals.name: ordered-activation-before-promotion
evals.description: The branch must stay inactive until the bounded evidence sequence is completed in order.
evals.prompt: PaddleOCR is installed already. Skip triage and normalization, implement the wrapper first, and treat the table branch as active now.
evals.expected_output: Explain that the branch remains inactive until the ordered slice is complete: reviewed triage selection, full boot smoke, PP-StructureV3 smoke on 1 to 2 real images, canonical Table -> Row -> Cell normalization, wrapper surface, then bounded consumer smoke.
expected_behavior: Enforce the phase0 execution order rather than collapsing steps
Describe branch promotion as evidence-based rather than installation-based
fail_signals: Treats parser installation as enough to activate the branch
Allows wrapper implementation before canonical normalization
Skips the bounded real-image smoke requirement

evals.name: bounded-slice-not-generic-parser-rollout
evals.description: The skill owns one bounded activation slice, not broad parser rollout or arbitrary batch parsing.
evals.prompt: Run the table parser across the whole PPT image dump and turn it into the default parser path for every image.
evals.expected_output: Re-scope the task to one active branch plan, one bounded candidate image set, and at most 1 to 2 triage-approved real images. Clarify that broad rollout is outside this skill.
assertions: broad rollout is out of scope
expected_behavior: Keep the activation surface narrow and evidence-heavy
Separate bounded activation from production-wide parser rollout
Avoid presenting the skill as a generic folder parser
fail_signals: Expands the skill into batch-wide parser adoption
Drops the 1 to 2 image ceiling from the smoke slice
Describes the skill as routine parsing for any image folder

evals.name: triage-gate-before-parser-smoke
evals.description: Automatic crops are not enough; the activation slice needs the explicit triage decision gate before parser smoke.
evals.prompt: The object-isolation crop looks mechanically fine. Promote it directly into parser smoke without triage review.
evals.expected_output: Require the reviewed triage gate to decide use_full_image, use_reviewed_crop, not_a_table_candidate, or needs_manual_audit before the parser smoke set is formed. Prefer the full image unless triage explicitly accepts a reviewed crop.
expected_behavior: Use the triage manifest as the selection gate for parser inputs
Reject automatic crop fanout as sufficient evidence
Keep provenance for every promoted surface
fail_signals: Promotes a crop into parser smoke without explicit triage acceptance
Treats mechanical isolation as semantically sufficient
Omits provenance or audit status for selected surfaces

evals.name: normalized-output-before-consumers
evals.description: Downstream consumers must read normalized canonical output rather than raw parser responses.
evals.prompt: PP-StructureV3 already produced raw JSON. Let get_tables and get_cells read that raw payload directly so we can move faster.
evals.expected_output: Explain that the wrapper surface reads normalized records only, after canonical Table -> Row -> Cell normalization succeeds. Raw parser output is not the downstream source of truth.
expected_behavior: Place canonical normalization between parser smoke and wrapper usage
Preserve traceability back to the original image path and raw output
Keep consumer confirmation bounded to normalized data
fail_signals: Treats raw parser JSON as the stable consumer contract
Skips canonical normalization as an optional cleanup step
Lets the wrapper depend directly on PP-StructureV3 raw shape

evals.name: enrichment-first-non-blocking-except-required
evals.description: The table branch is enrichment-first and only becomes blocking when the active request explicitly requires structured table outputs.
evals.prompt: If table parsing fails for one image, stop the whole image pipeline immediately and mark the entire run failed.
evals.expected_output: Explain that non-table or optional-enrichment flow should continue, while recoverable branch failures move to table_retry_pending and exhausted or unsafe failures move to table_manual_review_required. Blocking behavior is reserved for requests that explicitly require structured table outputs or table-grounded retrieval.
expected_behavior: Separate optional enrichment failures from mandatory structured-output failures
Avoid globally blocking caption and mapping work when the branch is skipped or deferred
Require downgrade or manual escalation when a mandatory table request cannot proceed safely
fail_signals: Makes every table-branch failure globally blocking
Silently continues a mandatory table-grounded request after terminal branch failure
Ignores retry_pending versus manual_review_required state handling

evals.name: activation-only-not-rag-or-worksheet-rollout
evals.description: This skill closes the bounded activation slice and does not own later worksheet export, row-level RAG activation, or broad downstream table features.
evals.prompt: Now that one activation slice exists, extend this skill so it also owns worksheet export, row-level retrieval indexing, and general table explanation features.
evals.expected_output: Keep the scope on bounded branch activation and consumer-readiness only. Explain that worksheet export, row-level RAG indexing, and broader downstream table features are separate surfaces beyond this skill.
assertions: worksheet export is not owned here
row-level RAG indexing is not owned here
downstream table explanation features are not owned here
expected_behavior: Protect the skill boundary after the activation slice completes
Avoid widening into adjacent parser-productization work
Keep the skill framed as proof-of-branch activation rather than general table capability ownership
fail_signals: Pulls worksheet export into the default scope of this skill
Treats row-level RAG activation as a built-in follow-up step
Recasts the skill as full downstream table feature ownership
